skill.name: plate-handler
skill.description: Implement Plate.js rich text editors for React apps, including compact comment editors and fuller document editors.
skill.summary: Build simple or detailed Plate.js rich text editors with shadcn UI components.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code, headings, and documentation text. The confirmed risks are legitimate but meaningful: the skill instructs agents to run external package installation commands and to write editor files, and it suggests optional localStorage persistence for editor content.

medium_findings.title: External Package Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: The Ruby backtick detections are false positives because the commands are Markdown examples, not executable Ruby. However, the skill tells agents to run pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add commands, which can download packages and modify project files from external sources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented setup commands, not hidden execution. They still create a real supply-chain and file-modification risk if run without user review.

low_findings.title: Optional Browser Storage Persistence
low_findings.description: The skill suggests syncing editor value with localStorage or form state. This is normal editor guidance, but localStorage can retain sensitive draft content longer than users expect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly names localStorage, but it is a design option rather than implemented data collection. The risk depends on the consuming application.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The referenced lines describe Plate.js, heading plugins, basic nodes, and shadcn UI installation. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing, ciphers, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found UI and documentation terms at the cited lines, not cryptographic code. There is no semantic evidence for weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detection Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The cited best-practice line discusses storing editor JSON and avoiding dangerouslySetInnerHTML. No evidence found of host inspection, environment enumeration, or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a rendering safety recommendation for editor content. It does not contain commands or logic that inspect the system.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote CLI Package Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add commands. Users should review package changes and lockfile updates before accepting generated edits.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The installation commands are explicit and central to the skill workflow. The risk is supply-chain exposure, not confirmed malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Browser Storage Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill mentions localStorage for editor state. Applications should avoid this option for private drafts unless retention and cleanup are explicit.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The storage mechanism is directly named, but it is only suggested as one possible state-management choice. The severity depends on application data sensitivity.

content.value_statement: Teams often need rich text editors that match their React and shadcn UI stack. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Plate.js setup, component choices, and editor patterns.
actual_capabilities: Chooses between compact and detailed Plate.js editor patterns.
Guides installation of Plate and shadcn UI editor components.
Outlines component paths for simple and detailed editor files.
Provides React and TypeScript examples for Plate editor setup.
Explains toolbar, plugin, and state-management choices for editors.
limitations: Requires a React project that can use Plate.js and shadcn UI.
May need package installation commands that modify project dependencies.
Does not verify compatibility with every Plate.js release.
Does not implement backend storage, validation, or access controls.

use_cases.title: Add Comment Formatting
use_cases.description: Create a small editor with basic marks for comments, chat inputs, or profile text.

use_cases.title: Build CMS Editing
use_cases.description: Create a larger editor with headings, blockquotes, lists, media options, and toolbar controls.

use_cases.title: Standardize Editor Components
use_cases.description: Place editor files in consistent paths and align them with shadcn UI conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Editor
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for short text fields.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plate-handler skill to add a simple Plate.js editor for comments. Keep formatting to bold, italic, and underline.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Detailed Editor
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plate-handler skill to add a detailed Plate.js editor for blog posts with headings, quotes, and toolbar controls.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt to Existing UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plate-handler skill to integrate Plate.js with the existing shadcn UI components and follow current component paths.

prompt_templates.title: Review Editor Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plate-handler skill to compare simple and detailed Plate.js editor options for this app, then recommend the lowest-complexity implementation.

output_examples.input: Add a compact editor to a chat composer.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan that installs required Plate components, creates a simple editor component, and wires basic formatting controls.

output_examples.input: Build a blog post editor with headings and quotes.
output_examples.output: A detailed editor plan with selected Plate plugins, toolbar controls, recommended file paths, and state-handling guidance.

output_examples.input: Decide which editor type fits a profile bio field.
output_examples.output: A recommendation for the small editor pattern, including which plugins to include and which document features to avoid.

best_practices: Review dependency and lockfile changes after running shadcn or pnpm commands.
Use the smallest editor feature set that satisfies the product requirement.
Store editor content in application state or backend storage when drafts may be sensitive.
anti_patterns: Do not add a full document editor for short comments or chat messages.
Do not persist sensitive editor drafts in localStorage without clear retention rules.
Do not render stored rich text as raw HTML without a trusted serialization path.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Plate.js rich text editors for React apps using shadcn UI components.

faq.question: Can it create both small and full editors?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes compact editors for short text and detailed editors for documents or CMS fields.

faq.question: Does it require installing packages?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes pnpm dlx shadcn commands that can add dependencies and files.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive drafts?
faq.answer: Use care. Avoid localStorage for sensitive content unless retention and cleanup are clearly defined.

faq.question: Does it include backend persistence?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on frontend editor components and state handling patterns.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
